quotes.quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.
quotes.rationale: Execution and scoring in a sandbox produce a score and logs that are routed back to guide subsequent generation, i.e. closed computational feedback.

quotes.quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).
quotes.rationale: Establishes the sandbox scoring is embedded in a search loop, confirming search-control decision role.

quotes.quote: The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.
quotes.rationale: A deterministic PUCT selection rule over empirical scores controls which node is expanded next; distinct mechanism from sandbox scoring itself.

quotes.quote: approach does not rely on beam search (which aggressively prunes unselected candidates at each depth layer) nor a (1 + 𝜆 ) evolution strategy (which maintains only the most recent generation and discards historical states).
quotes.rationale: Characterizes the search-control policy by contrast, and confirms historical nodes are retained rather than pruned.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Human experts judge whether code matches the requested algorithms; the packet reports no routing of this judgment back into the search, so it is terminal only.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Consistency of performance across replicate runs is a distinct reproducibility target judged from repeated computational runs, not from human artifact review.

policy_type: PUCT-based tree search balancing exploitation and exploration over LLM-generated code nodes
backtracking/branching from any historical node on plateau
memory_structure: global flat search tree of executed solutions with empirical scores and visit counts backpropagated to ancestors
parent code and score used to condition child generation

diagnostics.statement: No reliability study (discrimination, agreement, bias, or external validation) is reported for the sandbox score that drives search control; its fidelity as a proxy for the intended scientific quality is unassessed in the packet.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

diagnostics.consequence: Hill climbing may optimize toward score artifacts or sandbox-specific shortcuts without any reported check that the score tracks the intended property.

diagnostics.statement: Expert manual inspection reports that only 'nearly all' implementations adhered to the requested algorithms, indicating a residual set of non-compliant solutions, and this human judgment is terminal rather than routed back into the search.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

diagnostics.consequence: Specification-noncompliant solutions can be scored and retained by the search because compliance is checked only after the fact, outside the optimization loop.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports no novelty or prior-art assessment applied to the generated solutions, despite the search generating a 'novel child solution' at each expansion in a purely score-driven sense.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score.

diagnostics.consequence: Novelty relative to existing prior art is unverified in this source; 'novel' denotes only a new tree node, so novelty claims cannot be supported from the packet.

diagnostics.statement: The reliability checks reported (expert inspection and replicate-run consistency) are conducted within the reporting system's own evaluation; no independent replication by a separate team is reported.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1)

diagnostics.consequence: Reported performance rests on internal self-evaluation, so systematic internal error or setup-specific effects would not be detected.

diagnostics.statement: The packet describes the search-control machinery in detail but does not state what human authority, if any, governs generator, executor, memory, or policy configuration.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox.

diagnostics.consequence: The boundary between human-supplied task/idea inputs and autonomous system operation cannot be fixed, limiting attribution of results to the system.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

success_check.observable: Agreement between sandbox score ranking of solutions and an independently constructed held-out or external evaluation of the same solutions
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Rank correlation reported with confidence interval against a preregistered threshold, versus a shuffled-label baseline
success_check.evidence_needed: A property-specific external validation study of the sandbox scorer over a documented sample of generated solutions

exact_quotes.exact_quote: expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1)

success_check.observable: Whether specification-compliance verdicts are routed into node scoring or expansion eligibility rather than reported only after the run
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Rate of non-adherent solutions surviving to the reported final set, compared before and after routing
success_check.evidence_needed: Run logs showing compliance verdicts entering the search policy, plus paired non-adherence rates across matched runs

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score.

success_check.observable: Proportion of accepted solutions screened against a documented prior-art corpus with recorded verdicts
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: All reported headline solutions screened; screening recall measured on a seeded set of known prior methods
success_check.evidence_needed: A prior-art corpus, a documented matching procedure, and per-solution verdicts

exact_quotes.exact_quote: with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1)

success_check.observable: Reproduction of headline scores by a research team not involved in developing the system
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Replicated scores within a prespecified tolerance of originally reported scores
success_check.evidence_needed: An external replication report with released code, task definitions, and run configuration

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox.

success_check.observable: An explicit statement of which artifacts (task definition, scoring function, research ideas, search hyperparameters) are human-supplied versus system-generated
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every pipeline input attributed to a named role with no residual unattributed inputs
success_check.evidence_needed: A documented role-by-role authority table covering generator, executor, validator, memory, and policy
